steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The phrase “ratified in 1804” is a participial phrase (past participle + prepositional phrase) that comes between the subject “The 12th amendment” and the main verb “separated.”

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
A participial phrase between the subject and verb must be set off with commas on both sides.

Since the opening comma is already present, “1804” needs a comma to close it.

The other options (em dash, colon, or no punctuation) fail to properly mark the phrase's end.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
